name: auth
context: Cloudflare API authentication — tokens, keys, scopes, and permission configuration
info: Cloudflare provides two authentication methods: API Tokens (scoped, revocable, recommended) and the Global API Key (full account access, legacy). API Tokens are created in the Cloudflare dashboard under My Profile > API Tokens and carry specific permissions like 'Zone:DNS:Edit' or 'Account:Workers Scripts:Edit'. The token is passed as a Bearer token in the Authorization header. The Global API Key requires both the key and account email. Agents should always use API Tokens with minimal permissions. Wrangler authenticates via `wrangler login` (OAuth browser flow) or by setting `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` in the environment.
triggers: what permissions do i need for cloudflare
body:
# Cloudflare Authentication

All Cloudflare API calls require authentication. There are two methods: **API Tokens** (recommended) and the **Global API Key** (legacy).

## API Tokens (Recommended)

API Tokens are scoped, revocable credentials. Each token carries specific permissions and can be restricted to specific zones or accounts.

### Creating a Token

1. Go to [Cloudflare Dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens)
2. Click "Create Token"
3. Choose a template or create custom
4. Set permissions (e.g., `Zone:DNS:Edit`, `Account:Workers Scripts:Edit`)
5. Set zone/account restrictions
6. Create and copy the token (shown only once)

### Using API Tokens

```bash
# Via environment variable (recommended)
export CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"

# In API calls
curl -X GET "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json"
```

```javascript
// Node.js SDK
import Cloudflare from 'cloudflare';

const cf = new Cloudflare({
  apiToken: process.env.CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN,
});
```

### Common Permission Sets

| Task | Permissions Needed |
|------|--------------------|
| Deploy Workers | Account:Workers Scripts:Edit |
| Manage DNS | Zone:DNS:Edit |
| Manage R2 | Account:Workers R2 Storage:Edit |
| Manage KV | Account:Workers KV Storage:Edit |
| Deploy Pages | Account:Cloudflare Pages:Edit |
| Read analytics | Zone:Analytics:Read |
| Full admin | Account:Account Settings:Read + all zone permissions |

## Global API Key (Legacy)

The Global API Key provides full account access. It requires both the key and account email.

```bash
# NOT recommended — use API Tokens instead
curl -X GET "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones" \
  -H "X-Auth-Key: $CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY" \
  -H "X-Auth-Email: $CLOUDFLARE_EMAIL" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json"
```

**Warning:** The Global API Key has full account access and cannot be scoped. If leaked, an attacker has full control. Always prefer API Tokens.

## Wrangler Authentication

```bash
# Interactive OAuth login (opens browser)
wrangler login

# Or set token in environment
export CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"
export CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID="your-account-id"

# Verify authentication
wrangler whoami
```

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose | Required |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` | Scoped API token (preferred) | Yes (or API Key) |
| `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` | Account identifier | Yes for most operations |
| `CLOUDFLARE_API_KEY` | Global API Key (legacy) | No |
| `CLOUDFLARE_EMAIL` | Account email (with API Key) | Only with API Key |

## Finding Your Account ID

```bash
# Via API
curl -s "https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN" | jq '.result[0].id'

# In the dashboard: appears in the URL and on the Workers overview page
# Or via wrangler:
wrangler whoami
```

sections.title: API Tokens
sections.body: API Tokens are the recommended authentication method. They are scoped to specific permissions, can target specific zones or accounts, and are individually revocable. Create tokens at dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens. Pass the token as `Authorization: Bearer <token>` in HTTP headers or set `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` in the environment for Wrangler and SDKs.

sections.title: Common permission sets
sections.body: Workers deployment: `Account:Workers Scripts:Edit`. DNS management: `Zone:DNS:Edit`. R2 storage: `Account:Workers R2 Storage:Edit`. KV storage: `Account:Workers KV Storage:Edit`. Pages deployment: `Account:Cloudflare Pages:Edit`. For development, the 'Edit Cloudflare Workers' template covers Workers, KV, R2, and D1. Always use the minimum permissions required.

sections.title: Wrangler authentication
sections.body: Run `wrangler login` for interactive OAuth login that opens a browser. For CI/CD and agent use, set `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` as environment variables. Verify with `wrangler whoami`. Wrangler stores OAuth credentials in `~/.wrangler/config/default.toml`.

sections.title: Finding your Account ID
sections.body: The Account ID is required for most API calls and Wrangler operations. Find it in the Cloudflare dashboard URL (`dash.cloudflare.com/<account-id>`), on the Workers & Pages overview page sidebar, or via `wrangler whoami`. Set it as `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` in the environment or `account_id` in `wrangler.toml`.

related.note: Auth is required for all Cloudflare API operations
Wrangler consumes API tokens for deployment
Similar token-based auth model with scoped permissions
